title: New Case Results
description:
Seeking help add the new case results on their page please.
https://www.collinslaw.com/case-results/

Please follow the same format like the other blocks.


  • A $800,000 settlement against a Chicagoland assisted living facility who failed to properly secure our client’s electric wheelchair while transporting him in a medical transport bus. The bus driver stopped abruptly and caused our client to fall from his wheelchair, and the electric wheelchair to fall on top of him, fracturing his hip and leg, which required surgery to repair.

  • A $930,000 settlement involving a truck driver who died after the grab handle on the side of the truck’s cab broke away from the cab, causing the truck driver to fall backwards, sustaining fatal injuries.  The case was settled at mediation on behalf of the surviving family members.

  • A One Million Dollar judgement against a skateboard manufacturer.  Our client was riding an electric skateboard when the wheels malfunctioned, causing our client to be thrown off the board, sustaining multiple fractures that required surgical intervention. 

 
Thanks much!

CC

title: August New Firm News
description:
Seeking help post the “August newsletter” below to the firm news page please.
https://www.collinslaw.com/firm-news/ 
 
August 2025 Newsletter

Personal Injury Results

The Firm recovered over Three Million Dollars on behalf of our personal injury clients during the month of August. 

Cases included:
  • A $800,000 settlement against a Chicagoland assisted living facility who failed to properly secure our client’s electric wheelchair while transporting him in a medical transport bus. The bus driver stopped abruptly and caused our client to fall from his wheelchair, and the electric wheelchair to fall on top of him, fracturing his hip and leg, which required surgery to repair.
  • A $930,000 settlement involving a truck driver who died after the grab handle on the side of the truck’s cab broke away from the cab, causing the truck driver to fall backwards, sustaining fatal injuries.  The case was settled at mediation on behalf of the surviving family members.
  • A One Million Dollar judgement against a skateboard manufacturer.  Our client was riding an electric skateboard when the wheels malfunctioned, causing our client to be thrown off the board, sustaining multiple fractures that required surgical intervention. 

Business Litigation Results

The Firm was successful in a complete dismissal with prejudice for our client in a fraud and veil piercing lawsuit.

The Firm closed over $18 million in commercial transactions in August.


Thanks much!

CC

title: Change to Shawn’s profile
description:
Seeking help make the update for Shawn's profile page please.
https://www.collinslaw.com/shawn-m-collins/

Please add the following message: indicated by two green arrows in the screenshot

Text to add:

 
IN MEMORY OF SHAWN M. COLLINS
Shawn M. Collins
Founder/Partner
Dec. 8, 1957 – Dec. 15, 2024

Shawn will be remembered as a brilliant, tenacious, caring, and courageous attorney. A gifted speaker, litigator, and strategist, he dedicated his life to fighting for those without a voice against the powerful interests that harmed them. His contributions to The Collins Law Firm, which he founded in 1992, were immeasurable. His leadership fostered a culture built on the values of fierce advocacy and service to others. His deep commitment to the law was evident in everything he did--whether comforting a client, arguing a motion in court, or developing a case winning strategy. To the thousands of clients he helped achieve justice and secure clean air and water, he will be remembered as a modern-day Sampson against Goliath. To his colleagues, he will be remembered as a talented lawyer, mentor, and friend. And to his family, he will forever be a devoted husband, loving father, and the very personification of a "good man". We will continue to honor his legacy in the work we do and the people we help at Collins Law.


Thanks!

CC

title: New Firm News
description:
Request - please post the “July newsletter” below to the firm news page.
https://www.collinslaw.com/firm-news/ 

 
July Newsletter

Personal Injury Results

The Firm obtained a $2.8 Million Wrongful Death verdict in DuPage County.  Our client was a 72 year-old man with stage IV lung cancer who was receiving radiation therapy at Good Samaritan Hospital in Downers Grove. He was sedated for radiation treatment, but Defendant failed to secure his legs to the treatment table, allowing him to roll over in his sleep during treatment and fall to the floor, fracturing his neck and skull and suffering a brain bleed. He died from these injuries five days later. The jury deliberated for two hours before returning the verdict.
The firm settled an automobile accident case for $1.1 Million. Our client was hit head on and required a cervical fusion surgery.  The matter settled after party depositions, and before expert depositions. 
The firm settled a wrongful death claim on behalf of a woman who suffered fatal injuries as a result of a home fire.  She was a tenant in an attic apartment in Cook County and the property owners were negligent renting her an apartment that did not meet city codes, which prevented her from escaping the fire.  The matter settled for the policy limits of $300,000.00. 

In addition to the above mentioned cases, the Firm settled an additional $1.3 Million in auto cases during the month of July.

Business Litigation Results

The Firm recently completed a large seven-figure syndicated loan closing for a national bank for a South Carolina project. 

The Firm recently closed a $5.5 Million office complex.

The Firm’s recent Motion to Dismiss was granted for a multi-million dollar fraud claim against our client.
Link:

https://secure.campaigner.com/csb/Public/show/ceb8-2yqubw--1aozqp-7y8noc72


Thanks!

title: Update bio - John Risvold
description:
Request- please update bio for John Risvold. Please replace the current bio with the updated one provided by the client.
URL- https://www.collinslaw.com/john-d-risvold/ 

 
John Risvold is a nationally recognized personal injury trial lawyer and partner at The Collins Law Firm, P.C., based in the Chicago area. He represents individuals and families in catastrophic personal injury, medical malpractice, wrongful death, nursing home neglect, and high-stakes trucking and motor vehicle crash litigation. His focus includes traumatic brain injury, spinal cord injury, and complex multi-vehicle collisions. With over $37 million recovered for his clients, John is known for his relentless courtroom advocacy and results-driven approach.
 
A seasoned trial lawyer, John has successfully tried numerous cases to verdict in Illinois and litigated complex personal injury matters across the country. Before joining The Collins Law Firm, he defended major insurance carriers and multinational corporations in multimillion-dollar litigation. That experience gives him insider knowledge on how defendants and insurers assess, value, and fight serious injury cases—and he uses that insight to outmaneuver them in court.
 
Proven Results
 
John’s verdicts and settlements have earned national recognition:
  • Top 50 Verdict in Illinois – Bigoness v. Krane
  • Top 100 Verdict in Illinois – Johnson v. 53rd Woodlawn
  • Top 100 Motor Vehicle Accident Settlement in the U.S. – Moss v. Braun, et al.
 
Industry Recognition
 
  • Illinois Super Lawyers Rising Star, 2017–2025
  • Top 40 Under 40 Trial Lawyers – National Trial Lawyers
  • 10 Best Personal Injury Attorneys for Client Satisfaction – AIOPIA
  • Best Lawyers in America: Ones to Watch – Personal Injury Litigation
  • R. David Ray Excellence in Trial Advocacy Award
  • Order of the Barristers – Trial Advocacy Honor Society
 
John is an active member of the American Association for Justice (AAJ), Illinois Trial Lawyers Association (ITLA), Justice HQ, and the Chicago Bar Association. Through these organizations, he fights to protect access to the courts and preserve justice for the injured.
 
His work has been featured in ABC7 Chicago, Chicago Tribune, Super Lawyers Magazine, Huffington Post, WBBM Radio, Naperville Sun, and other media outlets.
 
John earned his B.A. and J.D. from the University of Missouri. He lives in the western suburbs of Chicago with his wife and three children.
 
Jurisdictions Admitted to Practice
  • Illinois
  • Missouri
  • U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois
  • U.S. District Court for the Central District of Illinois
  • U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Missouri
Professional & Bar Association Memberships
  • Chicago Bar Association
    Director – Young Lawyers Section, Chicago Bar Association
  • Illinois Trial Lawyers Association
    Amicus Curiae Committee Member
  • American Association for Justice
  • DuPage County Bar Association
Honors and Awards
  • CALI Excellence for the Future Award in Trial Practice
  • R. David Ray Award for Excellence in Trial Advocacy
  • 2017 Super Lawyers Rising Star
Fraternities / Sororities
  • Delta Tau Delta
Featured Cases and Matters
  • Confidential Settlement versus a Cook County Hospital
  • After her caesarian section surgery, a surgical sponge was left inside of a new mother. John was able to successfully negotiate a confidential settlement with the hospital and the OB/GYN on behalf of the client.
  • Policy Limits Settlement in Head on Car Crash
  • When the insurance company wouldn’t be fair, we took them to court. Following several months of litigation, after taking key depositions and proving fault, the insurance company agreed to settle the case for their full policy limits for our brain injured client.
 
Thanks much!

title: Add to "Firm News" page
description:
Seeking help post the three newsletters to be posted on the "Firm News" page -  https://www.collinslaw.com/firm-news/ 

1. April 2025 Newsletter 

Personal Injury Results
The Firm recovered $700,000.00 during the month of April.  Cases include: a pre-suit settlement against a drunk driver who struck a pedestrian who sustained catastrophic injuries as a result; a pre-suit settlement for a client who was attacked by a dog at her apartment complex; and a jury trial verdict for a client who sustained injuries after a young driver backed into him while he was walking in a parking lot.  The total verdict was in the six figures, despite no offer by the defense.

Business Litigation Results
The Firm recovered $5 million for breach of oral contract.
The Firm obtained $861,000 settlement for breach of contract in sale of stock.
The Firm won dismissal for a bank client accused of breaching a $3 million loan.
 
Speaking Engagements
The Firm is pleased to announce that Partner John Risvold is speaking at the Chicago Bar Association Solo & Small Firm Conference on Using Social Media to Grow Your Law Practice on May 2.

2. January 2025 Newsletter

As we look forward to 2025, we would like to take a moment to reflect on the successes we achieved for our clients in 2024. We thank all our clients for the trust you have shown in us and wish you all the best in the new year.
 
Environmental Law
After 4 years of litigation, partner Edward Manzke and a team of attorneys from leading environmental law firms entered into a partial settlement of the Chemtool class action case for $94.5 million on behalf of thousands of class members who were affected by the chemical explosion and resulting toxic plume at the Chemtool facility in Rockton, IL. The partial settlement is believed to be the largest settlement in a chemical explosion case.  The case continues as to another defendant.
 
Personal Injury Law
The personal injury team of partners Ed Manzke and  John Risvold, and associate Christopher Gauthier had a very successful year, recovering verdicts and settlements in the amount of $14.5 million for their injured clients. Highlights include:
  • $2 million – Birth injury wrongful death settlement against an Illinois Hospital for the delayed diagnosis of fetal distress and delay in ordering and performing a cesarean section surgery resulting in the death of the newborn.
  • $1.6 million – jury verdict in DuPage County in a rear end crash resulting in four spine surgeries. State Farm refused to pay the judgment and was sued for insurance bad faith.
  •  $1.5 million - settlement for an automobile case involving a drunk driver who was driving the wrong way down the street and crashed into our client’s vehicle head-on. 
  •  $1.5 million - settlement for the driver of an automobile struck by a semi-truck driver who lost control of his truck. 
  • $900,000 – settlement for a developmentally disabled woman who was assaulted at a Chicagoland assisted living facility.
  •  $550,000 – settlement for a man who slipped and fell on ice at his apartment complex, resulting in a mild traumatic brain injury. 
  • $450,000 – settlement for a man injured during a fireworks display.
  •  $360,000 – policy limits settlement for a passenger who was injured when the driver of his vehicle ran a stop sign and crashed into a ditch while driving home from their high school prom.
 
Commercial Law
The commercial law practice with Partner Rob Dawidiuk and senior associate Len Hudson also had a successful year, including the following victories:
 Trials / Arbitrations:
  • Won complete defense verdict in international nine-figure lawsuit in which the plaintiffs were seeking control of all of our clients properties and accounts located throughout the world. 
  • Won complete defense verdict in favor of an Illinois bank on multiple mechanic lien claims. 
  • Won plaintiff verdict on real estate litigation in favor of our client, the owner of a shopping center.
  • Won arbitration award in excess of six-figures in favor of national health care organization
  • Secured over $2 Million for minority shareholder in shareholder oppression litigation.

Summary Judgement or Motion Wins:
  • Full dismissal of all claims on defamation lawsuit on behalf of our client.
  • Complete defense win on all claims by motion to dismiss for a 7-figure breach of contract case improperly alleged against our national client.
  • Summary Judgment granted for over $4 Million in favor of our client, a financial institution.
  • Summary Judgment granted for over $3 Million in favor of our client, a financial institution against defendants located in Florida and Iowa.
  • Won dismissal of a $3 million fraud claim on behalf of individuals sued by a bank.

Transactions:
  • Documented and closed over $150 Million in commercial loan transactions for our lending institution clients.
  • Closed in excess of $110 Million in acquisitions.
 
Sexual Assault Law
The sexual assault practice area led by associate Maggie Galka obtained a $700,000 settlement from a college on behalf of a survivor of a sexual assault that took place on the college’s campus. The survivor brought claims against the college based on allegations that the college mishandled her report of sexual assault and failed to prevent the sexual assault where other students had previously reported sexual misconduct by the same student-perpetrator.

3. December 2024

It is with profound sadness that we share the news that Shawn Collins, our founder, leader, and friend has passed away. 

Shawn’s career has been dedicated to fighting for people whose water, air or soil had been contaminated by polluting corporations.  Shawn’s passion for helping victims seek justice has been instilled in each member of Collins Law, where we will continue Shawn’s legacy of fighting for the “little guy and girl”. 

To read more about Shawn’s remarkable life, you can view Shawn’s obituary here:
https://www.legacy.com/us/obituaries/dailyherald/name/shawn-collins-obituary?id=57038855&fbclid=IwY2xjawHg_YdleHRuA2FlbQIxMAABHTSdOg8nrcM5EVMs8nqTDTY4YlZBx3zgEuMp1lQMW69Ela2qb7syd0DtFQ_aem_23K9KcY-bY9So4EuM1BtmQ

And an article in the Daily Herald here:
https://www.dailyherald.com/20241216/news/such-a-force-for-good-renowned-environmental-lawyer-collins-who-fought-for-pollution-victims-dies/?fbclid=IwY2xjawHg_illeHRuA2FlbQIxMAABHefa1e8KRUeqUytlumcSaDBdQkGrazGS42lMdiHSuWrOtOuOgIF0zdyn0w_aem_IB4NSInL6UAwXkiYB1mTsA

title: Need your input: Revision of Environmental lawyer pages
description:
Meg sent over a request to have us help them determine the best way to accomplish pruning some environmental content. Can you guys review and provide us with the best course of action moving forward: 

Here's what she said: 

Shawn's loss has a huge effect on our environmental team. 

In response to this, we think that the firm should streamline the environmental practice area section of our website and narrow our focus to the kinds of cases the team would like in the future. So, we are wondering if it would be OK to remove some environmental pages that we don't need any more.

We hope this would have the added benefit of preventing those environmental pages from competing with our personal injury practice area pages. To that end, I wonder if we can removing the following pages:

  • Dacthal Lawyers page 
  • Illinois Landfill Contamination Lawyers page.
  • Soil Contamination Lawyers page
  • Nitrate and Nitrite Drinking Water Contamination Lawyers page
  • What You Need to Know about  MTBE page
We don't see these pages getting us cases, and they clutter up our website. Removing them will help focus our website. We would only do this, of course, if it would not hurt our SEO in some fashion. Can you advise us?

FYI: We will be adding a Chemical Explosion Lawyers page to the website, since that is an area of continuing interest for us. 

Please let us know your thoughts.

title: New Case Type Landing Page: SA
description:
Hi ! 

There is a new case type that The Collins Firm wants to add to their site and have requested a landing page to be added to the site. We spoke about this on our reporting call w/ them yesterday. I told them that we want our SEO team to review the landing page and strategy needed to help them rank for SA cases as well. 

  1. Here's what they provided for the landing page: 
    1. Attached is our new Sexual Assault Attorneys practice area page. Please choose some appropriate and considerate photos to go with the page.

      Please remove the "Abuse of the Disabled" link on the main practice area page (https://www.collinslaw.com/practice-areas/#personal_injury) and add a link to this new SA Attorneys page instead.

      ***There will still be a link to the "Abuse of the Disabled" page on the Personal Injury Practice Area page (https://www.collinslaw.com/personal-injury-lawyer/). The vast majority of these cases don't get filed because the abuse is perpetrated by a family member with no money, and many others involve mental health issues and no way of proving any of the abuse.  
    2. Copy for the landing page: 
Please let me know: 
  • Once you review the content they provided, who do I assign this to first - seo or dev team? 
  • Is there anything we need to be aware of with their requests about "Abuse of the Disabled" 
  • Is this request in scope for new case types that the client is expanding into? 
If we need to have a call around this, let me know and I'll do my best to be available early tomorrow morning to discuss. 

Thank you!

title: Add New Press Release

title: Add Attorney:
description:
- tagging you guys as is out.

Attorney Name & Details: 
Anna Sheridan
Associate attorney
630-527-1784 Ext. 224
asheridan@collinslaw.com


Bio:
Anna Sheridan joined the firm in 2024 as an associate attorney. Her practice focuses on banking, finance, and real estate law, as well as business and contract law. She is part of the firm’s business law team, representing financial institutions in transactional matters.
 
Anna graduated cum laude from Northern Illinois University College of Law, where she concentrated on business law and served as the treasurer for NIU’s Business Law Society. While in law school, Anna interned at a business law firm for two years, gaining significant experience in contract drafting and redlining. She spent her spare time focusing on pro-bono work and was awarded the Pro-Bono Service Recognition from her law school.
 
Prior to attending law school, Anna graduated cum laude from Florida International University, where she earned her BBA, and served as a Guardian ad Litem volunteer.
 
Anna now lives in Lisle with her husband and enjoys spending time with family and friends.

title: Time cost and feasibility of building this kind of progressive form...
assignees: Chris Traynor, Clayton Coombs, Justyna Acosta, Keith Bruss, Sky Ortigas
description:
Please find the email request below...here is my analysis of what is required:

This link will take you to the Lawbrokr proposal for Collins law firm. About half way down the page there's a slide deck and then a personalized video at the foot of the page. They only give a broad overview of the product and unfortunately none of the custom steps that Collins will need on their form. I understand that this will have to form a part of the project discovery process if we have one.

There is a design from Lawbrokr below but I don't think this has been done in conjunction with Collins and will probably change. Tagging in here to look at that.

Also tagging to give his two cents on the actual benefits of this as I don't think they're as clear cut as Lawbrokr might make them out to be. Does it actually increase submissions and decrease drop offs? I'm not so sure. I think it would likely improve the intake process though, for the firm and the prospect.

For now I need a discussion of feasibility and usefulness and also a time cost to design, develop and launch this. There will likely be a planning meeting needed with the firm also that needs to be priced for.

------------------

This is what Lawbrokr offer vs what I think we could possibly do:

1. Progressive Form - This would have to be decided but could we try and replicate Lawbrokr's 9-page form for the moment? Form would be on-site obviously, not redirecting use off-site.
2. Campaign Builder
3. Journey Builder
4. Responses Report - submissions dumped into a spreadsheet and sent to a specific email address. Possible intake software form integration also.
5. Data Insights


Button opens form from anywhere on site:


Data collection on the form:

Page 1: Type of case


Page 2: Liability


Page 3: Location of injury - with autocomplete city/state dropdown



Page 4: Premises liability 1


Page 5 Premises Liability 2



Page 6 Medical Assistance



Page 7 Evidence



Page 8 Contact info



Page 9 Summary of data, with submit button


Tagging for visibility and comment.

ORIGINAL EMAIL REQUEST -
--------------------------------

Hi Corey,

Can our website designer make our "contact us form" interactive like the example in LawBrokr?

Lawbrokr makes the "contact us form" on our website interactive. Lawbrokr claim that this increases the number of people who actually complete the form. Right now, our contact us form has a high bounce rate (people who leave without filling it out). An interactive "contact us" format is based on the way people like to act online, pushing buttons, answering multiple choice questions, etc. 


This link shows the Lawbrokr product.
https://lawbrokr.dock.us/collins-law-WtP7KXz2LrrZ/Lf0GXlR6jBvx

LawBrokr also offers some other features that I'm not sure we need , but I like the idea of an interactive "contact us form".

Thanks,

Margaret Collins
The Collins Law Firm
1770 Park Street, Suite 200
Naperville, IL 60563

description:
Let me know how to move forward these requests to Lionhead OR if a discussion with the client needs to be had.

, I'm not sure if you saw her email, but I've forwarded it to you guys. Could you guys please review her requests below regarding the drop-down menu's and main interior practice area page.

I wanted to get your feedback on it before passing some of these on to Lionhead to modify. I have created a task for Lionhead to review her questions and jump on a few of Meg's requests.

Thank you!

copied from the email 



Farther down the page, Lionhead again changed things we didn't ask them to change. Below, we didn't ask them to add personal injury or abuse of the disabled to these practice area boxes. Do we need 8 boxes here? If so, let us know and we can decide what the 2 new boxes should be.
I included this first one to make sure you guys agree that it should be only the original 6 practice areas featured on the home page before I ask Lionhead to switch it back. 




They also changed the drop-down menu at the top without us asking us how to do it. If you are going to have separate drop-down boxes for personal injury, environmental, and business, then each separate drop-down menu should have a link at the bottom that says, "View All". 


In addition, the Practice areas included on the drop-down menu should be:
Environmental:  Toxic Chemical  Exposure, Chemical Vapor Intrusion, Roundup Cancer Cases, Ethylene Oxide Contamination, Dacthal Weedkiller Exposure (We need this page on the website when it goes live),  "View All".
Personal Injury: the categories are fine. Just add a "View All" link.
Business Law: Breach of Contract, Partnership Disputes, Finance and Banking, Commercial Real Estate, Business Litigation, "View All"

On the Main Interior Practice Area Page: 


Under Personal injury cases we handle, we need to replace "Airbag injuries" with "Paragard IUD Injuries" and add "Hair Relaxer Lawsuits",  which was a blog post, but which should actually be a practice area page. https://www.collinslaw.com/blog/hair-relaxer-lawsuit-attorneys-in-illinois/ (We have several Paragard and Hair Relaxer cases but have never gotten an airbag injury case.)


Under Environmental Cases we handle, we need to replace the box labeled "Pesticide/Herbicide Information" with "Dacthal Weedkiller Exposure" linked to the new page above. (We are actively pursuing leads for these cases.)


Where did all of our toxic informational pages (See below) that were linked on the right side of our environmental practice area pages go? We need these links back on the environmental pages. 
Toxic Chemical Information
  • What You Need to Know About Benzene
  • What You Need to Know About Methyl Tertiary Butyl Ether (MTBE)
  • What You Need to Know About Polychlorinated Biphenyls (PCBs)
  • What You Need to Know About Perchloroethylene (PCE)

  • What You Need to Know About PFAS, PFOA, and PFOS

Toxic Metal Information
  • What You Need to Know About Arsenic
Pesticide/Herbicide Information
  • What You Need to Know About Paraquat
  • What You Need to Know About Enlist Duo
  • What You Need to Know About Glyphosate


Where did these pages go? They used to be linked on the right side of our main car accident page.

title: Establish LSA Access
description:
1. Pull the CID from the onboarding form (found under "CLIENT WORKBOOK" door on the project's front page, then the "Project Details" tab. Should be ROW 28.)
  2. Send a management request using the Google Ads interface. TUTORIAL
  3. Validate the request was sent. TUTORIAL
  4. Send the client an email advising them to look out for the management request. Provide them with THIS TUTORIAL to reference should they encounter any issues. You can find the client's email address under the "CLIENT WORKBOOK" door on the project's front page, then the "Project Details" tab. Should be ROW 5. Comb through this sheet to ensure all points of contact are CC'd.

description:
These will be found in the signed proposal (contract) you uploaded earlier to the CONTRACT DOCS folder in the Client Folder. If they're not there then this is not currently being used:

  1. Column X - Contract Amount (WEBDEV) - Get this from the signed proposal uploaded to the CONTRACT DOCS in Client folder. This is used in Website Development projects only.
  2. Column Y - LSA Management Fee  - not currently used
  3. Column Z - SmartAds Management Fee - not currently used
  4. Column AA - SmartAds Budget - not currently used
  5. Column AB - Current Monthly Contract Amount ($) - Get this from the signed proposal uploaded to the CONTRACT DOCS in Client folder. This is used for Unfair Advantage SEO projects only.
  6. Column  AC - Current Supplementary Budget ($) - not currently used
  7. Column AD - Current monthly link budget ($) - not currently used
  8. Column AE - Current monthly content budget ($) - not currently used
  9. Column AF - Current monthly content budget (words) - Get this from the signed proposal uploaded to the CONTRACT DOCS in Client folder. Used in Unfair Advantage SEO projects only.

description: Remember to add SoW to the top of the Basecamp project. This is to be manually checked after onboarding call but generally should include SoW, Locations, Service/Case-types & notes from submitted Enquiry Form.

title: Raise Project number in Project Register
description:
1. Change column A from Inactive to Active

  2. Change column E from Active to Inactive

  3. Add entry to column B:
    3.1 Copy Enquiry Number from column E and 'paste special'>'values only' into column B
    3.2 Remove "E" prefix and replace with:
         J, if operation (has no agreed end date, SEO, LSA etc.)
         P, if project (has agreed end date, website development etc.)
         3.21 Change box background color:
                      Green: SEO
                      Yellow: LSA
                      Blue: WebDev

  4. Add project start date in column C - Should usually be today's date
         An easy way to do this would be to copy the date from the cell above, double click and choose today's date from the calendar date picker

  5. Select the cell above in column D and pull the handle down.
         This will automatically add the correct project title column D. This is what you use for the project number in the project doc titles
